footer.description: Intelligent incident investigation for engineering and L2/L3 support teams.

cofounderCta.headline: Let's Talk About Your Incident Workflow
cofounderCta.description: We're Engineering Managers and AI Specialists who spent years investigating incidents across disconnected tools. We built CauseFlow because we lived the same pain you're dealing with right now.
cofounderCta.personalNote: No sales pitch — just a conversation about your team's biggest pain points.

hero.badge: The first AI-powered incident investigation and L2/L3 support solution
hero.headline: Investigate production incidents in minutes, not hours
hero.subheadline: The first platform combining AI-powered incident investigation with L2/L3 support. CauseFlow connects to Slack, GitHub, Jira, CloudWatch and HubSpot, cross-references all data sources and delivers the root cause with fix recommendations — all in under 5 minutes.
hero.metaDescription: AI-powered incident investigation bridging engineering and L2/L3 support. CauseFlow cross-references Slack, GitHub, Jira, CloudWatch and HubSpot to find root causes in minutes.
hero.trustText: Be among the first to investigate incidents with AI. Launching March 2026.

howItWorks.title: How CauseFlow investigates your problems
howItWorks.subtitle: From any problem source, through all your tools, to the root cause —in minutes.

receive.title: Receives the problem from any source
receive.description: Via web interface, Slack, Jira or customer email. Describe the problem and CauseFlow starts investigating immediately.

investigate.title: Investigates all sources automatically
investigate.description: Connects to GitHub (commits, PRs, releases), CloudWatch (error logs), Jira (tickets), Slack (messages) and HubSpot (customer impact) simultaneously.

identify.title: Identifies root cause with evidence
identify.description: Cross-references logs, commits, tickets and metrics to deliver the root cause with confidence score and event timeline.

recommend.title: Recommends specific fixes
recommend.description: Suggests the exact fix: deploy revert, config adjustment, code fix PR. All with human-in-the-loop.

learn.title: Learns from every investigation
learn.description: Builds a Knowledge Base that accelerates future resolutions of recurring problems.

audit.title: Complete and transparent audit trail
audit.description: Every agent action is recorded in an immutable log visible to the customer. You see exactly what the agent did, when, and what data it accessed.

crossTool.title: The first agent that crosses technical data with business tools
crossTool.description: While competitors focus only on infrastructure, CauseFlow investigates the complete problem context. Receive a customer report 'I think my data was deleted' and CauseFlow automatically queries the customer's account in the database, checks audit logs for recent changes, determines if a deployment caused the issue, and generates both a technical fix and an explanation for the customer.
crossTool.vizFixDescription: Deployment v2.4.1 introduced a race condition in the data sync service, causing intermittent record deletion.

usageModes.title: Use CauseFlow in the way that makes most sense for your team

slack.title: Via Slack
slack.description: Send a message in Slack describing the problem and the agent investigates right in the incident channel. Results appear as a thread.

jira.title: Via Jira/Trello
jira.description: Assign a card to CauseFlow and it investigates automatically. The final report becomes a comment on the card.

web.title: Via Web Interface
web.description: Access the dashboard, describe the problem, and follow the investigation in real-time with visible audit trail.

api.title: Via API
api.description: Integrate CauseFlow into your alerting pipeline. Trigger investigations via REST API with result webhooks.

mcp.title: Via MCP Server
mcp.description: Use CauseFlow as an MCP tool inside your AI coding agent or IDE.

agent.title: Via AI Agent
agent.description: Let the CauseFlow agent work autonomously within your workflow.

security.title: Enterprise Security from Day 1

onDemandReading.title: On-demand reading
onDemandReading.description: The agent reads, analyzes and discards. Your data never leaves the perimeter.

isolatedEncryption.title: Isolated encryption
isolatedEncryption.description: Each tenant has individual KMS encryption via AWS.

immutableAuditTrail.title: Immutable audit trail
immutableAuditTrail.description: Every agent action is logged and visible to the customer.

noExternalTraining.title: No external training
noExternalTraining.description: Your data is never used to train third-party models.

readOnlyDefault.title: Read-only by default
readOnlyDefault.description: The agent never writes without explicit approval (human-in-the-loop).

rightToDeletion.title: Right to deletion
rightToDeletion.description: Delete all your data at any time.

security.badges: LGPD and GDPR compliant since launch. SOC 2 Type II roadmap.

ctaFinal.title: Ready to investigate problems in minutes?
ctaFinal.subtitle: Be among the first to investigate incidents with AI. Early access members get 3 free investigations/month at launch.
ctaFinal.ctaSecondary: Or contact us for a personalized walkthrough

whyDifferent.title: Why CauseFlow is Different
whyDifferent.subtitle: We're not another enterprise tool. We're built by engineers, for engineers.

builtByEM.title: Built by Those Who Lived the Problem
builtByEM.description: We spent years as Engineering Managers and AI Specialists investigating incidents with broken toolchains. CauseFlow was born from that shared frustration — built by the people who lived it.

minutesNotHours.title: Investigation in Minutes, Not Hours
minutesNotHours.description: AI-powered analysis connects the dots across your entire stack — logs, metrics, alerts, and code changes — in minutes instead of hours.

crossTool.title: Cross-Tool Intelligence
crossTool.description: CauseFlow integrates with your existing tools and correlates data across them. No more switching between 10 tabs to find the root cause.

builtForStartups.title: Built for Startups and SMBs
builtForStartups.description: Enterprise-grade investigation without enterprise pricing. Designed for teams of 2-50 engineers who need to move fast.

hero.title: Intelligent Investigation for Engineering Teams
hero.subtitle: CauseFlow connects to the tools your team already uses, automatically investigates logs, commits, tickets and metrics, and delivers the root cause in minutes.

phase1.title: Phase 1 —Assisted Investigation + Remediation

step1.title: Receives the problem
step1.description: Via web interface, Slack message, Jira/Trello card or customer email. The user describes the problem in natural language and the agent starts the investigation.

step2.title: Connects to all sources
step2.description: Slack (messages from #incidents channel), GitHub (commits, PRs, recent releases), Jira (related tickets), CloudWatch (error logs), HubSpot (affected customer data). All in parallel.

step3.title: Analyzes and correlates
step3.description: Cross-references data from all sources using multi-model LLM with intelligent routing by complexity. Generates hypotheses, tests against evidence, classifies with confidence score.

step4.title: Delivers complete report
step4.description: Probable root cause + confidence score (0-100%) + chronological event timeline + specific fix recommendations + customer impact (if applicable).

phase1.remediation: With user approval, the agent executes the fix plan: generates PRs on GitHub, executes kubectl commands, remediation scripts, deploy revert. Always with human-in-the-loop before any destructive action.

phase2.title: Phase 2 —Intelligent Knowledge Base
phase2.headline: The more you use it, the faster it resolves
phase2.description: The system learns from every resolved investigation, building a Knowledge Base that maps service dependencies and blast radius. When a similar problem is detected, CauseFlow suggests the previous solution immediately —recurring problems resolved in seconds, not hours.

phase3.title: Phase 3 —Autonomous Remediation
phase3.headline: Auto-healing with guardrails
phase3.description: Automatic correction with configurable approval: deploy revert, config adjustment, automatic scaling. Integration marketplace and autonomous L1 ticket resolution.

auditTrail.title: See exactly what the agent did
auditTrail.subtitle: Total transparency. Every agent action is recorded in an immutable log visible to you.

architecture.title: Technical Architecture
architecture.connectivity: Connectivity layer: MCP servers (10,000+ available in the ecosystem, adopted by OpenAI, Google, Microsoft)
architecture.core: Proprietary core: Planning engine, hypothesis generation, learning and Knowledge Base
architecture.llm: LLM Gateway: Intelligent router that selects the optimal model by task complexity
architecture.security: Security: AWS Bedrock (ISO/IEC 42001), KMS per-tenant, PII Gateway (Presidio)

hero.title: Simple pricing. Pay for what you use.
hero.subtitle: Launching March 2026. Join the early access list and be the first to try CauseFlow.

comparison.title: Why pay per user if only 2-3 people investigate incidents?

roi.title: ROI Calculator
roi.incidentsLabel: How many incidents/investigations does your team do per month?
roi.durationLabel: How long does each investigation take on average?
roi.engineersLabel: How many engineers on the team?

q1.question: What counts as an investigation?
q1.answer: Every time CauseFlow receives a problem and runs a complete investigation (connecting to sources, analyzing data, generating report) counts as 1 investigation.

q2.question: Can I change plans?
q2.answer: Yes, at any time. Upgrade is immediate, downgrade on next cycle.

q3.question: Do I need a credit card for the Free plan?
q3.answer: No. The Free plan doesn't require a credit card.

q4.question: Is there an annual discount?
q4.answer: Yes. Pre-paid credits offer 10-20% progressive discount.

q5.question: What if I exceed the limit?
q5.answer: You pay the overage price of your plan per additional investigation. No surprises —you configure consumption alerts.

q6.question: How does it work for Brazilian companies?
q6.answer: We accept international payment via Stripe. LGPD compliant since launch.

hero.title: Security you can audit
hero.subtitle: Your data never leaves the perimeter. The agent reads on demand, analyzes and discards. Everything it did is recorded in an immutable audit trail visible to you.

onDemandReading.title: On-demand reading, no persistence
onDemandReading.description: The agent reads data only during active investigation. After analysis, data is discarded. We don't store raw customer data.
onDemandReading.technicalDetail: Data read on demand, analyzed in-memory, discarded after investigation completion.

leastPrivilege.title: Least privilege access
leastPrivilege.description: Each integration uses read-only credentials with minimum scope via OAuth. The agent never has write access unless explicitly authorized.
leastPrivilege.technicalDetail: OAuth scopes limited to read-only; write access requires explicit human approval.

noWriteDefault.title: No writing by default
noWriteDefault.description: The agent is read-only. Remediation actions require explicit user approval (human-in-the-loop) before any destructive action.
noWriteDefault.technicalDetail: All write operations gated behind human-in-the-loop approval flow.

tenantIsolation.title: Tenant isolation
tenantIsolation.description: Each customer has individual KMS encryption via AWS. Data is never mixed between customers. LLM calls contain data from exactly one tenant.
tenantIsolation.technicalDetail: Per-tenant KMS keys, isolated ECS tasks, single-tenant LLM contexts.

noCrossTraining.title: No cross-training
noCrossTraining.description: Customer data is never used to train models for other customers. Fine-tuning is exclusive per account when applicable.
noCrossTraining.technicalDetail: Zero cross-customer data sharing; per-account fine-tuning isolation.

immutableAuditTrail.title: Immutable audit trail
immutableAuditTrail.description: Each investigation generates detailed log in S3 with Object Lock (WORM). The log is visible to the customer and cannot be altered.
immutableAuditTrail.technicalDetail: S3 Object Lock (WORM), includes: sources accessed, data read, tokens processed, result.

lgpd.name: LGPD
lgpd.details: KMS per-tenant + TTL + right-to-delete. Data subject requests fulfilled in 15 days. Breach notification in 72h.

gdpr.name: GDPR
gdpr.details: Same mechanisms cover GDPR. Standard Contractual Clauses for cross-border transfers.

soc2Type1.name: SOC 2 Type I
soc2Type1.details: Automated compliance platform. Timeline aligned with SOC 2 readiness milestones.

soc2Type2.name: SOC 2 Type II
soc2Type2.details: Full audit cycle with independent assessor. Builds on Type I controls.

architecture.title: Security Architecture
architecture.waf: Client (HTTPS) -> Web Application Firewall -> Load Balancer -> ECS Tasks (private subnets)
architecture.nat: ECS Tasks -> Network Gateway -> External APIs (Slack, GitHub, etc.)
architecture.credentials: Credentials in AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation
architecture.database: Managed Database/NoSQL Database in private subnet with no internet exposure
architecture.logs: All logs centralized in Monitoring & Alerting + Immutable Object Storage
architecture.encryption: Data at rest: AES-256 per-tenant encryption keys. Data in transit: TLS 1.3

isolation.title: Data Isolation (Multi-tenancy)
isolation.application: Tenant ID required in every query, log and record. LLM calls containing data from exactly one tenant.
isolation.database: Database with Row Level Security (RLS) by tenant_id.
isolation.vectorDb: Partitioned vector storage with hard isolation per tenant. Designed to scale to millions of tenants.
isolation.infrastructure: Each investigation runs in ephemeral container with restricted permissions. Container destroyed upon completion.
isolation.piiGateway: Microsoft Presidio detects and anonymizes emails, phones, SSNs, cards. Reversible anonymization.
isolation.encryption: Data at rest: AES-256 per-tenant encryption keys. Data in transit: TLS 1.3.

bedrock.title: AWS Bedrock as LLM Provider
bedrock.noTraining: AWS does not use customer data to train models
bedrock.zeroAccess: Model providers have zero access to prompts/completions

hero.title: Connect your tools in minutes
hero.subtitle: CauseFlow integrates with the tools your team already uses. OAuth or API key —no complex setup, no agent to install.

setupMinutes.title: Setup in minutes
setupMinutes.description: OAuth or API key —no complex setup, no agent to install. Average setup: 3 minutes per integration.

readOnly.title: Read-only by default
readOnly.description: The agent only reads. Never modifies your data without explicit approval.

onlyNeeded.title: Only what's needed
onlyNeeded.description: Fetches only the data relevant to the active investigation. No mass collection.

noSensitiveData.title: No sensitive data
noSensitiveData.description: Automatic PII/sensitive data redaction via Microsoft Presidio.

technicalBusiness.title: Technical + Business
technicalBusiness.description: First platform to cross technical tools (GitHub, CloudWatch) with CRM (HubSpot) and support.

hero.mission: We believe investigating production problems shouldn't consume hours of your team's time. CauseFlow exists so engineers can focus on building, not firefighting.

deploymentApproaches.sectionSubtitle: Same AI. Same root cause. You decide how your data flows.

connected.title: Connected Mode
connected.description: Direct read-only access to your systems via AWS IAM Role or OAuth2. Fastest setup, real-time analysis.
features: Read-only access — no write permissions required
Real-time analysis across all connected tools
Full audit trail of every access

privacyPreserving.title: Privacy-Preserving Mode
privacyPreserving.description: A lightweight Docker agent runs inside your infrastructure — on-premise or private cloud. It processes, masks, and anonymizes all sensitive data (PII, API keys, debug logs) before anything leaves your environment. This is edge-based data minimization aligned with GDPR Article 25 and LGPD — a Privacy-Enhancing Technology (PET) that lets your team use AI-powered investigation without exposing sensitive production data.
features: Raw data never leaves your environment
GDPR Article 25 & LGPD compliant data minimization
Reversible mapping stays in your infrastructure

product.title: How CauseFlow Connects to Your Systems
product.subtitle: Choose full access for maximum speed, or privacy-preserving mode for maximum data protection. Both deliver the same root cause.

security.title: Privacy-Preserving Architecture: Your Data Never Leaves
security.description: CauseFlow is the only incident investigation platform that deploys a Privacy-Enhancing Technology (PET) directly in your infrastructure. A Docker agent processes, masks, and anonymizes sensitive data at the edge — hostnames, IPs, user IDs, API keys, service names — before any transmission. Your raw data never leaves your security perimeter. The AI cloud receives only what it needs for root cause analysis, with sensitive identifiers replaced by opaque tokens.
guarantees: Reversible mapping stays in your infrastructure

compare.calloutTitle: The Only Platform with Privacy-Preserving Architecture
compare.calloutDescription: Investigate incidents with edge-based data minimization — your raw data never leaves your infrastructure. A Privacy-Enhancing Technology (PET) included free on every plan. No other incident investigation platform offers this level of data protection.
